title: Tests — update both fixture suites to v2 + add v1-receipt assertion
description: Lockstep test fixtures with the new wire shape: every `version: 1` literal becomes `version: 2`, every `narrow:` payload field becomes `entries:`, and the TypeScript Payload type renames `narrow: NarrowEntry[]` → `entries: NarrowEntry[]`. Add ONE new test per file asserting the v1-receipt stance (passing a v1 payload returns `[]` and writes the expected stderr breadcrumb).

files.target: lines 30-43 (Payload type), 85-96 (makePayload helper), 175-200 (defensive missing-field tests), and every test using makePayload (search the whole file)
files.change: Rename TS type field: `narrow: NarrowEntry[]` → `entries: NarrowEntry[]` (line 41); change `version: 1` → `version: 2` in the Payload type (line 38) and in makePayload (line 90); rename helper return field `narrow,` → `entries,` (line 94); update the defensive test at lines 180-182 from `payload.narrow is missing` → `payload.entries is missing` and adjust the test JSON `{ version: 1, ... }` → `{ version: 2, ... }`. Add a NEW test block: `describe('knowledge-hint-broad.cjs — v1-receipt stance (protocol v2 cut)', () => { it('returns [] and emits one stderr breadcrumb on version=1 payload', () => { const stderrChunks: string[] = []; const fakeStderr = { write: (c: string) => { stderrChunks.push(c); return true; } }; const lines = hook.renderSummary({ version: 1, revision_hash: 'x', target_paths: ['**'], narrow: [makeEntry('a','decision','proven','x')], broad_count: 1 } as any); expect(lines).toEqual([]); /* stderr breadcrumb assertion: if renderSummary writes via process.stderr the test must spy on it; if the project test convention is to plumb stderr via env, adjust accordingly */ }); });` — note: the stderr-spy mechanism may need to use `vi.spyOn(process.stderr, 'write')` since renderSummary in TASK-004 writes via `process.stderr.write` directly.

files.target: lines 39-46 (Payload type), 138-147 (makeCliPayload helper), and every fixture using `narrow:`
files.change: Same rename as broad: `narrow: NarrowEntry[]` → `entries: NarrowEntry[]` in Payload type (line 42); `version: 1` → `version: 2` (lines 39, 142); helper field `narrow,` → `entries,` (line 145); cascading update of every test fixture using makeCliPayload. Add the parallel `describe('knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs — v1-receipt stance (protocol v2 cut)', ...)` block with the same v1-payload assertion.

implementation: Open packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-broad.test.ts; rename Payload type fields at lines 38-43 (`version: 1` → `version: 2`, `narrow:` → `entries:`)

Update the defensive missing-field test at lines 180-182 from `payload.narrow is missing` → `payload.entries is missing`; adjust the test JSON object literal to use `version: 2` (the test asserts `renderSummary({ version: 2, revision_hash: 'x' })` returns `[]` because `entries` is missing — same defensive behavior, new field name)
Search the rest of the file for any remaining `narrow:` literals or `version: 1` literals using `grep -n 'narrow:\|version: 1' packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-broad.test.ts` and patch each

Add a new describe block at the END of the file: `describe('knowledge-hint-broad.cjs — v1-receipt stance (protocol v2 cut)', () => { ... })` with one test `it('returns [] and emits stderr breadcrumb on version=1 payload')` — use `vi.spyOn(process.stderr, 'write')` to capture the breadcrumb; assert lines === [] AND spy was called once with a string matching `/version=1 unsupported/`
Repeat for packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-narrow.test.ts: rename type, rename helper, search-and-replace remaining literals, add the parallel v1-receipt describe block
Run `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest run knowledge-hint-broad knowledge-hint-narrow` and confirm ALL tests pass — this is the integration moment for the entire rc
Commit message: `test(rc18): update knowledge-hint test fixtures to protocol v2 + add v1-receipt assertions`

reference.pattern: Lockstep fixture migration — the test files are the contract evidence; if they pass against the v2 emitter+consumers the protocol cut is complete
reference.examples: The new v1-receipt test is THE convergence proof for TASK-001's stance decision — without it the stance is documented but not enforced

criteria: Zero remaining `version: 1` literals in either test file (verified via `! grep -E 'version: 1[^0-9]' packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-*.test.ts`)

Zero remaining `narrow:` payload field references in either test file (verified via `! grep -E '^\s*narrow:' packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-*.test.ts` — note the anchor avoids false positives on local var names)

Both test files contain a new describe block titled `v1-receipt stance (protocol v2 cut)` with at least one `it(...)` test asserting `lines === []` and `stderr.write` called once with a `version=1 unsupported` substring

Test command: `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest run knowledge-hint-broad knowledge-hint-narrow` exits 0 with all tests passing (this is the GREEN signal for the entire protocol cut — TASK-002/003/004 individual builds may have had passing TS checks but a passing test suite is the integration proof)
Behavioral expectation: every fixture in both test files uses the v2 wire shape; the v1-receipt stance is enforced by an automated test, not just a doc claim

Field-name consistency check: emitter (`entries`) ↔ both consumers (`payload.entries`) ↔ both test fixtures (`entries:`) — full chain aligned, verifiable by `! grep -rn 'payload\.narrow' packages/cli/` returning zero results
Version-receipt stance check: the new v1-receipt test passes (renderSummary returns [] AND stderr breadcrumb fires)
Single git commit with conventional commits `test(rc18): ...` prefix

rationale.chosen_approach: Defer all test changes to a single integration commit AFTER all 3 production-code tasks land — this guarantees the test suite is either red (broken) or green (fully migrated), never partially passing. Bundling broad+narrow tests in one commit honors the per-file commit convention loosely (test pair is one logical unit)
alternatives_considered: Split into TASK-005a (broad tests) + TASK-005b (narrow tests)
Co-commit each test file with its corresponding consumer (TASK-003 + narrow-tests, TASK-004 + broad-tests)
decision_factors: Single GREEN signal at end (CI clarity)
Test-suite logical pair (broad+narrow are conceptually one contract)
Avoids race with parallel TASK-002/003/004 — tests can only land after all 3 do
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly larger commit (~2 files); CI is red until this commit lands — accepted because rc.18 ships SOLO and the chain is local

manual_checks: Run `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest run knowledge-hint-broad knowledge-hint-narrow` and confirm 100% pass rate including the two new v1-receipt tests
Run `grep -rn 'payload\.narrow' packages/cli/` and confirm zero matches anywhere in the package
Read both new v1-receipt describe blocks and confirm they assert BOTH `lines === []` AND a stderr breadcrumb spy match
success_metrics: Both test files migrated to v2 in lockstep
Two new v1-receipt tests added (one per file), both passing
unit: renderSummary writes stderr breadcrumb on version=1 payload (spy match)
integration: Full broad+narrow test suite green against v2 emitter + v2 consumers
